chunks.text: Ptplot FAQ Ptplot FAQ Below are some answers to frequently asked questions about ptplot. This page contains links to documents about Ptplot internals and infrastructure. Contents 1. General 1.1 What Is Ptplot? 1.2 How do I pronounce 'ptplot'? 1.3 Wha

platforms does ptplot run under? 2.3 What do I need to Install Ptplot? 2.4 Troubleshooting 3. How do I . . . 3.1 Plot my own data? 3.2 How do I plot binary data? 3.3 How do I plot log axes? 1. General 1.1 What Is Ptplot? Ptplot is a Java 2-D graphin

l 1.1 What Is Ptplot? Ptplot is a Java 2-D graphing component. Ptplot can be used in applets and applications. The top-level Ptplot page describes Ptplot further. 1.2 How do I pronounce 'ptplot'? pee-tee-plot. Unlike with Ptolemy, the initial p in

e-tee-plot. Unlike with Ptolemy, the initial p in the pt is pronounced. 1.3 What is the copyright? Ptplot is released under the fairly liberal UC Berkeley copyright. Most files have the copyright at the top, for example, see Plot.java 1.4 How do I c

the top, for example, see Plot.java 1.4 How do I contact the authors? The primary author of Ptplot is Professor Edward A. Lee. Christopher Hylands is a coauthor. William Wu contributed code. You can contact the authors via ptplot at ptolemy.eecs.ber

contact the authors via ptplot at ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu 1.5 Is there a mailing list? We maintain the ptplot-announce alias for announcing new ptplot releases. This alias is for Ptplot announcments only. Thus, you cannot post to this group. To

s only. Thus, you cannot post to this group. To subscribe to this group, send email to ptplot-announce-request at ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu with the word subscribe in the body, not the header. 2. Installation questions 2.1 How do I download Ptplot

nstallation questions 2.1 How do I download Ptplot? http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptplot.html has links to the tar and zip files. 2.2 What platforms does Ptplot run under? Ptplot was develop under JDK1.1.4. The only part of Ptplot that requi

nder JDK1.1.4. The only part of Ptplot that requires JDK1.1 is the printing method in the Pxgraph class and the Message class, which is used by the Pxgraph class. The rest of the code should compile under JDK1.0.2 2.3 What do I need to Install Ptpl

under JDK1.0.2 2.3 What do I need to Install Ptplot? To use Ptplot, you will need the Java Development kit (JDK), which can be downloaded from http://www.javasoft.com . You can also run Pplot as an applet in your local browser. 2.4 Troubleshooting T

pplet in your local browser. 2.4 Troubleshooting The Ptplot Troubleshooting guide has a few hints. You might find useful information on the Ptplot homepage at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/java/ptplot . If you are really stumped, you can send mail

lot . If you are really stumped, you can send mail to cxh at eecs . Your mail should include: What version of Ptplot you are running. What platform you are running under (Solaris, Windows etc.) Exactly how to reproduce the bug. 3. How do I . . . 3.1

y how to reproduce the bug. 3. How do I . . . 3.1 How do I plot my own data? There are several ways to plot data using ptplot. One is to create a datafile that consists of datasets and x,y pairs See: demo/bargraph.plt Binary data files are also supp

demo/bargraph.plt Binary data files are also supported, see 3.2 How do I plot binary data? Another ways it to call plot().addPoint . The Fourier Series demo uses this methodd. The PlotFourierSeries class at demo/PlotFourierSeries.java is what actua

class at demo/PlotFourierSeries.java is what actually does the plotting. You can also plot data dynamically by creating a class that has a addPoints() method which gets called by the PlotLive class. The demo/Live.html demo illustrates live plotting.

The demo/Live.html demo illustrates live plotting. The PlotLiveDemo class at demo/PlotLiveDemo.java is what actually does the plotting. 3.2 How do I plot binary data? Ptplot can plot binary datafiles. The format was originally created by Joe Buck fo

The format was originally created by Joe Buck for the X11 pxgraph program. For details on the format, see the description of the -binary option in Pxgraph.java . You might find the javadoc Pxgraph documentation easier to read. 3.3 How do I plot lo

documentation easier to read. 3.3 How do I plot log axes? Ptplot1.1 does not support Log axes. We hope to support log axes in the future, many people have asked for this feature. Ptplot infrastructure Copyright © 1997, The Regents of the University
